metadata.title: Demonstrate the Solve-Verify Asymmetry with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. The domain ratios stuck with me — legal at seventy-two thousand x, finance at ten thousand eight hundred, and the code-with-tests exception where verify time drops at or below produce time. I want to turn that exception into a design principle. Help me: (1) take one of my own AI-assisted tasks and estimate its solve and verify times honestly, (2) design an automated verification layer that runs in seconds the way a test suite does, and (3) have Claude tell me whether my task even ADMITS such a layer or is structurally verification-bound.

props.command: The domain ratios stuck with me — legal at seventy-two thousand x, finance at ten thousand eight hundred, and the code-with-tests exception where verify time drops at or below produce time. I want to turn that exception into a design principle. Help me: (1) take one of my own AI-assisted tasks and estimate its solve and verify times honestly, (2) design an automated verification layer that runs in seconds the way a test suite does, and (3) have Claude tell me whether my task even ADMITS such a layer or is structurally verification-bound.
props.segment: Demonstrate the Solve-Verify Asymmetry with Claude

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. Claude's solve speed doubles roughly every eighteen months. Your verification speed is approximately constant. The gap is widening — and the only defense is to get better at the verify side.

props.command: Demonstrate the Solve-Verify Asymmetry with Claude Code
props.segment: Demonstrate the Solve-Verify Asymmetry with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Your job is not to solve faster — Claude has that side. Your job is to verify better. If you compete on solve speed you lose, and you should. If you abandon verify, the build ships with whatever Claude got wrong.

The same scaffolding principle that keeps a student from outsourcing their thinking protects a musician from outsourcing their voice. When Claude drafts, you judge. When Claude suggests a pitch angle, you decide if it's true. The tool that flags 'this is AI doing your creative work for you' is the tool that keeps your music yours.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: We ask Claude to write a top-three GPA ranking function with tie-breaking by last name.

props.command:
claude "Write top_three_by_gpa(students) -> list:
  sort by GPA descending
  tie-breaking: alphabetical by last_name ascending
  return top 3 students"

beats.narration_text: Claude writes the function in 8 seconds. It looks correct. The sort key is clean, the code is readable. The tie-breaking bug is invisible — until the probe.

props.code:
# gpa_sort.py — Claude Code output (8 seconds)
def top_three_by_gpa(students):
    """Return top 3 students by GPA.
    Tie-breaking: alphabetical by last_name ascending.
    """
    sorted_students = sorted(
        students,
        key=lambda s: (-s['gpa'], s['last_name'])
    )
    return sorted_students[:3]

# Looks correct. Clean sort key.
# Bug: last_name tie-breaking only applies when GPAs are equal.
# Test case needed: two students with identical GPA.
props.title: gpa_sort.py

beats.narration_text: Solve time: 8 seconds. Verify time: 4 minutes. Same task — the gap is 30x. And verify catches the bug that 8 seconds of solve couldn't self-identify.
We ask Claude to audit its own function for tie-breaking correctness.

props.command: claude "Audit top_three_by_gpa for tie-breaking correctness.\nDoes it correctly break ties alphabetically by last_name ascending?"

beats.narration_text: The gap is widening every model generation. Solve speed grows; verify speed doesn't. The ratio is the risk.

The solve-verify asymmetry is structural: Claude solves by pattern completion, which is fast and fluency-optimized. Verification requires deliberate judgment, which scales with human effort. The gap widens every model generation.
Next: label the five supervisory capacities in a real build session.

The gap widens every model generation. Next: label the five supervisory capacities in a real build session.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Demonstrate the Solve-Verify Asymmetry with Claude Code] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
